cross_links: Self-similarity/recurrence matrices overlap heavily with the MIR 'music structure & self-similarity (Foote novelty)' facet — the recurrence plot of a whole song IS a self-similarity matrix, and the Foote novelty kernel on its diagonal does structural segmentation.

Delay-embedding lag tau and the correlation dimension both rest on autocorrelation/mutual-information statistics, linking to any spectral/cepstral facet that already computes autocorrelation (e.g. pitch detection) — tau can be shared.

The audio-forced attractor (technique 3) is a particle/agent dynamical system, overlapping the 'GPU particle systems / flow-field & physics-driven visuals' facet (compute-shader position/velocity buffers, additive-blend trails).
The stereo goniometer and cross-recurrence (technique 6) connect to any 'stereo field / spatial audio imaging' facet and to phase-vocoder/phase-coherence work.

Autonomous threshold selection (fixed recurrence-rate epsilon, running-percentile feature normalization) is a reusable self-scaling primitive that any facet's auto-ranging/auto-dimensioning layer can adopt.
Correlation dimension and Lyapunov exponent provide a scalar 'complexity/chaos' control bus that can modulate parameters in spectral, particle, and color-mapping facets app-wide.

facet: Physical wave phenomena & cymatics — Chladni plate eigenmodes, driven plate response, membrane Bessel modes, and Faraday parametric waves as audio-driven visualizers
summary: Cymatics turns a sound's spectrum into the spatial standing-wave geometry it would excite on a real physical resonator, revealing structure (which modes a tone activates, how close two partials are to a shared eigenfrequency, the precise nodal topology) that is invisible in a spectrogram. The host app already has "basic Chladni" — almost certainly the closed-form square-plate nodal formula cos(nπx)cos(mπy) − cos(mπx)cos(nπy) = 0, which is only an idealized center-clamped approximation. The real depth lives in three upgrades: (1) solving the true Kirchhoff–Love biharmonic eigenvalue problem D∇⁴w = ρhω²w with free edges via FEM/spectral methods to get physically correct (not idealized) modes; (2) replacing single-mode display with the DRIVEN steady-state response — a Lorentzian-weighted modal superposition Σ φₙ(x)φₙ(x₀)/(ωₙ²−ω²+2iζωₙω) — so the audio spectrum itself selects and blends modes, with near-resonant modes blooming and beating; (3) Faraday parametric waves, where the surface responds at HALF the drive frequency through a damped Mathieu/Floquet instability, giving square/hexagon/quasi-pattern selection. The strongest autonomous-dimensioning story is here: the eigenspectrum {ωₙ} is computed once, then the audio's instantaneous spectrum drives mode weights, so the visualization self-tunes its modal content, wavelength, and pattern symmetry directly from the sound with zero manual parameter setting.

techniques.name: Driven Kirchhoff–Love plate response (Lorentzian modal superposition) — the true upgrade over basic Chladni
techniques.one_line: Instead of showing one idealized eigenmode, render the steady-state displacement of a harmonically driven plate as a complex Lorentzian-weighted sum of real eigenmodes, letting the live audio spectrum select and blend modes and bloom at resonances.
techniques.the_math: Governing PDE (Kirchhoff–Love thin plate, transverse displacement w): D∇⁴w + ρh ∂²w/∂t² = q(x,y,t), with flexural rigidity D = E h³ / [12(1−ν²)], ∇⁴ = (∂²/∂x²+∂²/∂y²)² the biharmonic operator, ρ density, h thickness, ν Poisson ratio. Free-vibration eigenproblem: D∇⁴φₙ = ρh ωₙ² φₙ ⇒ ∇⁴φₙ = kₙ⁴ φₙ with kₙ⁴ = ρh ωₙ²/D, so ωₙ = kₙ² √(D/ρh). Eigenmodes {φₙ} are orthonormal: ∫_Ω φₘφₙ ρh dA = δₘₙ. Steady-state DRIVEN response to harmonic point/area excitation f(x)e^{iωt} (ω = 2π·audio-frequency), with modal damping ζₙ: w(x,ω) = Σₙ [φₙ(x) Fₙ] / (ωₙ² − ω² + 2 i ζₙ ωₙ ω), where Fₙ = ∫_Ω f(x)φₙ(x) dA is the modal force (for a point drive at x₀: Fₙ = φₙ(x₀)). The visualized field is the real instantaneous surface u(x,t) = Re{ w(x,ω) e^{iωt} }; the Chladni nodal set is {x : |w(x,ω)| small}. Audio coupling: drive with the whole spectrum — w(x) = Σ_f A(f) Σₙ φₙ(x)φₙ(x₀)/(ωₙ²−(2πf)²+2iζₙωₙ(2πf)), A(f) = FFT magnitude bin. Near a resonance ω→ωₙ the denominator → 2iζₙωₙ², so that mode's amplitude ∝ 1/(2ζₙωₙ²) blooms — low damping ⇒ sharp, dramatic mode selection.
techniques.what_it_reveals: Reveals WHICH physical modes a sound actually excites and how strongly, not just its spectrum: two tones equidistant from a shared eigenfrequency produce visible beating in the pattern; a chord lights up a superposition of nodal geometries; pitch glides sweep the pattern continuously through mode crossings (anticrossings where degenerate modes hybridize become visible as the pattern rotates/morphs). The damping ζₙ controls resonance sharpness — you literally see Q-factor as how cleanly one pattern dominates. This is causal physics (this sound → this plate would do this), impossible to read from a spectrogram.
techniques.why_breathtaking: At resonance the nodal lines snap into crisp, almost crystalline symmetry, then dissolve into shimmering interference as the audio moves off-resonance; sustained chords produce living, breathing lattices that beat and rotate. Rendering thousands of sand particles that drift down the displacement gradient toward nodes (∂particle/∂t ∝ −∇|u|² ) adds granular, physical texture — sand cascading into pattern is mesmerizing.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Maximal. Eigenpairs {ωₙ, φₙ} are precomputed ONCE for the plate geometry (basis is fixed). At runtime the live FFT magnitude spectrum A(f) is literally the input vector — it auto-selects modal weights via the Lorentzian, so modal content, effective wavelength, and symmetry all self-scale to the sound with zero tuning. Map analysis sample-rate / fundamental to the plate's frequency-axis scaling so a sound's pitch range fills the available eigenspectrum; choose the number of retained modes N from a cumulative-energy threshold (keep modes until Σ captured weight > 99%).
techniques.implementation_notes: Two-phase: (offline/once) solve the biharmonic eigenproblem on the chosen domain — for a rectangle with free edges use a Rayleigh–Ritz / spectral expansion in products of free-free beam functions (Warburton's classical method) or a small FEM (Argyris/Hermite plate elements) giving a generalized eigenproblem K φ = ω² M φ; solve with a sparse symmetric eigensolver. In Rust: nalgebra/faer for dense small grids (a 60×60 grid → 3600 DOF, fully tractable), or build K,M as sparse and use a Lanczos/LOBPCG crate; precompute the first ~60–200 modes. (realtime) the driven sum is just N complex Lorentzian-weighted adds per pixel — trivially a wgpu fragment shader: upload φₙ as a texture array (or evaluate analytic modes in-shader), pass per-mode complex weights as a uniform buffer updated each audio frame from the FFT. 60–120 FPS easily. Gotcha: free-edge plate modes have NO closed form (the idealized cos·cos−cos·cos is only the center-clamped square approximation the app likely uses) — you must solve numerically to be physically honest. rustfft already present covers the audio side.

references.title: Exploring the resonant vibration of thin plates: Reconstruction of Chladni patterns and determination of resonant wave numbers (Tseng et al., JASA 137, 2015)
Resolving the formation of modern Chladni figures (EPL / ResearchGate)
The Fundamentals of Modal Testing — Application Note 243-3 (HP/Agilent): FRF as modal sum H(ω)=Σ φφ/((ωn²−ω²)+j2ζωnω)
Vibration of plates — Wikipedia (Kirchhoff–Love equation, D, BCs)

techniques.name: Closed-form rectangular plate eigenmodes (simply-supported) + idealized square-Chladni nodal curve
techniques.one_line: The analytically exact mode shapes and frequencies for a simply-supported rectangular plate, plus the classic center-driven square-plate Chladni nodal formula — instant, no eigensolver, good for a fast/fallback layer.
techniques.the_math: Simply-supported rectangular plate a×b, exact solution: φ_{mn}(x,y) = sin(mπx/a) sin(nπy/b), natural angular frequencies ω_{mn} = π² (m²/a² + n²/b²) √(D/ρh), m,n = 1,2,3,…  (Note the (1−ν²) inside D carries Poisson's ratio; for free edges this closed form FAILS and you need the numerical method above.) Idealized center-clamped square Chladni figure (the standard 'basic Chladni' formula): nodal set is the zero level-set of f(x,y) = cos(nπx/L)cos(mπy/L) − cos(mπx/L)cos(nπy/L), drawn as the contour f=0; superposing the two index orderings enforces the symmetry of a centrally driven square. Animated/driven version on a grid: z(x,y,t) = sin(nπx/L)sin(mπy/L)cos(2πf t), nodal lines where the spatial factor = 0.
techniques.what_it_reveals: Maps an excitation frequency to its mode indices (m,n): the count of nodal lines in each direction is a direct, countable readout of the mode order, so the visual literally spells out the harmonic index a tone is hitting. Degenerate pairs (m,n)/(n,m) at equal frequency reveal the square's symmetry and, when mixed, the family of rotated/superposed Chladni figures.
techniques.why_breathtaking: Clean, iconic, instantly readable geometric lattices — the textbook cymatics look, crisp and high-contrast. Cheap enough to render hundreds of overlaid contour layers or a dense particle field at high FPS.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Invert the frequency formula to pick (m,n) automatically from the audio's dominant frequency: given target f, choose integer (m,n) minimizing |ω_{mn} − 2πf|, so the pattern order self-scales to pitch. Plate size L can auto-set so the app's fundamental maps to the (1,1) mode and the Nyquist/top of range maps to a chosen max order, keeping nodal density perceptually bounded across the whole pitch range.
techniques.implementation_notes: Trivial in Rust/egui or a wgpu shader — evaluate the analytic field per pixel, marching-squares for crisp nodal contours, or threshold |z|<ε for a glow band. No precompute, no solver. Ideal as the lightweight default and as a visual cross-check that the numerical free-edge solver is behaving. This is almost certainly what the host app's 'basic Chladni mode' already does; keep it as the fast path and gate the true-eigenmode path behind it.

references.title: Chladni patterns — DynamicMath (square & circular nodal formulas)
Vibration of plates — Wikipedia (simply-supported rectangular ω_mn = (m²/a²+n²/b²)π²√(D/ρh))
CymaticsVisualizer (square z=sin(nπx/L)sin(mπy/L)cos(2πft); circular Bessel approx)

techniques.name: Circular membrane Bessel eigenmodes (drumhead cymatics)
techniques.one_line: The exact Bessel-function standing-wave modes of a circular membrane give the canonical nodal-diameter / nodal-circle drum patterns, with a closed-form inharmonic spectrum that visibly distinguishes a drum from a string.
techniques.the_math: 2D wave equation in polar coords: ∂²u/∂t² = c²(∂²u/∂r² + (1/r)∂u/∂r + (1/r²)∂²u/∂θ²), c² = T/σ (tension over areal density), fixed rim u(a,θ,t)=0. Separation u = R(r)Θ(θ)T(t) gives mode shapes u_{mn}(r,θ) = J_m(k_{mn} r)(C cos mθ + D sin mθ), with J_m the Bessel function of the first kind and the rim condition J_m(k_{mn} a) = 0 ⇒ k_{mn} = j_{mn}/a, where j_{mn} is the n-th positive zero of J_m. Natural frequencies: ω_{mn} = c k_{mn} = (j_{mn}/a)√(T/σ). m = number of nodal diameters, n = number of nodal circles. First zeros: j_{01}=2.4048, j_{11}=3.8317, j_{21}=5.1356, j_{02}=5.5201, j_{31}=6.3802, j_{12}=7.0156… ⇒ inharmonic frequency ratios 1 : 1.594 : 2.136 : 2.296 : 2.653 : 2.918 … (NOT integer multiples — this is why drums lack clear pitch). Driven version uses the same Lorentzian modal sum as technique 1 with these analytic φ_{mn}.
techniques.what_it_reveals: Exposes the inharmonic spectrum of membranes directly: the visual ratios 1, 1.59, 2.14… are the literal eigenvalue ratios, so a drum hit lights up modes that a harmonic (string/pipe) sound never would. Reveals azimuthal symmetry (m nodal diameters) and radial structure (n nodal circles) of whatever frequency is driving — a clean decomposition into angular×radial order.
techniques.why_breathtaking: The rotationally symmetric flower/target patterns are gorgeous and instantly 'cymatic'; spinning superpositions of cos mθ and sin mθ degenerate pairs produce rotating petals. Particle sand on a driven membrane pooling into concentric rings and radial spokes is striking.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Bessel zeros j_{mn} are universal constants — tabulate the first few hundred once. Auto-select (m,n) for the driving frequency by nearest eigenfrequency; auto-scale radius a (or c) so the audio fundamental maps to the (0,1) breathing mode. The radial resolution can adapt to the highest active n (refine the grid only as far as the active mode's nodal-circle count demands).
techniques.implementation_notes: Need Bessel J_m evaluation — use the `libm`/`special`/`bessel` Rust crates or a small in-house series+asymptotic implementation; zeros j_{mn} can be hard-coded from tables or found by Newton on J_m. Evaluate analytic modes per pixel on a polar or Cartesian grid in a wgpu shader (precompute J_m on a 1D texture lookup keyed by k_{mn}r for speed). Fully realtime. Gotcha: handle the m≥1 degeneracy (cos/sin pair) to allow rotating modes; pick phase from audio (e.g. stereo or instantaneous-phase) for direction.

references.title: Vibration of a circular membrane — Wikipedia (full derivation, ω_mn, J_m(λ)=0)
Vibration modes of a circular drum — Euphonics (ka zeros 2.405, 3.832, 5.136; mode classification)
Vibrational Mode Shapes of a Circular Membrane — Dan Russell, Penn State

techniques.name: Faraday parametric waves (subharmonic surface patterns via damped Mathieu/Floquet)
techniques.one_line: A vertically driven fluid surface answers at HALF the drive frequency through a parametric (Mathieu) instability, spontaneously selecting stripes, squares, hexagons, or 8/12-fold quasipatterns — a fundamentally different, emergent class of audio-driven pattern.
techniques.the_math: Each surface Fourier mode of wavenumber k has amplitude a_k(t) obeying a damped Mathieu / parametric oscillator equation under vertical forcing acceleration a_drive = F cos(Ωt) (Ω = 2π·audio drive freq): d²a_k/dt² + 2γ_k da_k/dt + ω_k²[1 − (F/g) cos(Ωt)] a_k = 0, with viscous damping γ_k = 2νk² and the gravity–capillary dispersion relation ω_k² = (g k + (σ/ρ) k³) tanh(k h) (g gravity, σ surface tension, ρ density, h depth). Floquet/Mathieu analysis: the primary (most unstable) tongue is SUBHARMONIC — the surface responds at Ω/2, i.e. the selected wavenumber k_c satisfies ω_{k_c} = Ω/2, giving (Ω/2)² = g k_c + (σ/ρ) k_c³ (deep water) — this sets the pattern wavelength λ_c = 2π/k_c. Instability threshold acceleration (low-viscosity estimate) F_c ≈ (something like) 2 γ_{k_c} Ω / ... ∝ ν — i.e. forcing must overcome damping. Pattern SELECTION comes from the weakly-nonlinear standing-wave amplitude equations Ȧ_j = μ A_j − (g₀|A_j|² + Σ_{i≠j} g(θ_{ij})|A_i|²) A_j over the resonant wavevector set; the sign/size of the angular coupling g(θ) decides stripes vs squares vs hexagons vs quasipatterns (high viscosity/high f → stripes; mixed capillary-gravity → hexagons & higher symmetry; gravity regime → stripes again).
techniques.what_it_reveals: Reveals a sound's energy as an EMERGENT symmetry, not a fixed lattice: the pattern's wavelength encodes the drive frequency through the dispersion relation (λ_c shrinks as f rises — capillary k³ regime), and the chosen symmetry (stripe/square/hexagon/quasicrystal) is a nonlinear fingerprint of amplitude and frequency. The half-frequency response makes a subharmonic visible — a tone produces a pattern oscillating at f/2, a genuinely non-obvious physical fact about parametric resonance.
techniques.why_breathtaking: This is the most spectacular cymatics class: living, self-organizing surfaces that ripple, flip between symmetries, and form 8- and 12-fold quasicrystalline patterns near threshold — organic and alive in a way fixed modal patterns are not. Onset is a sudden bifurcation: silence→flat, then a threshold crossing erupts into structure.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Strongest emergent self-scaling of all: the wavelength λ_c = 2π/k_c is computed directly from the drive frequency via the dispersion relation, so the spatial scale auto-tunes to pitch with no parameter; the symmetry self-selects from the amplitude equations as loudness (F) crosses the damping-set threshold, so quiet→no pattern, louder→pattern, even louder→symmetry transitions — the visualization's dimensionality (effective number of active wavevectors / rotational order) grows with the audio energy automatically. You can map RMS loudness → F and dominant frequency → Ω and let the physics dimension everything else.
techniques.implementation_notes: Two realtime routes. (a) Physics-faithful but heavier: solve the Floquet/Mathieu problem per-frame for the threshold and most-unstable k_c (a small generalized eigenvalue problem in a truncated Fourier-in-time basis — Kumar–Tuckerman method), then advance a 2D wavefield. (b) Realtime-friendly: precompute k_c(Ω) and threshold from the dispersion relation, then run a GPU reaction–diffusion-style amplitude-equation / Swift–Hohenberg surrogate ∂_t u = [ε − (∇² + k_c²)²]u − u³ on a wgpu texture — this naturally produces the stripe/hex/quasipattern zoo at scale k_c and runs at 60+ FPS. Gotchas: pattern selection is sensitive (the cubic coupling coefficients need tuning to hit hexagons vs squares); use the dispersion relation to drive k_c from audio and let ε ∝ (loudness − threshold). Surface-tension/depth params σ,ρ,h are free 'material' knobs the user can preset.

references.title: Kumar & Tuckerman, Parametric instability of the interface between two fluids (J. Fluid Mech. 1994) — Floquet/damped-Mathieu threshold
Amplitude equations and pattern selection in Faraday waves (arXiv patt-sol/9702002, Chen & Viñals)
Pattern formation in Faraday instability — experimental validation of theoretical models (Phil. Trans. R. Soc. A, 2023; PMC)
Faraday wave — Wikipedia (subharmonic Ω/2 response, parametric resonance)

techniques.name: Nodal-set extraction & particle 'sand' dynamics (the cymatics renderer)
techniques.one_line: The shared rendering layer that turns any of the above displacement fields u(x,t) into the visible Chladni image — crisp nodal contours plus thousands of GPU particles that migrate down the displacement gradient and pile up on the nodes.
techniques.the_math: Nodal set N = { x : u(x,t) = 0 } (or |w(x,ω)| < ε for the driven complex field); extract with marching-squares on the level set, or render an emissive band g(x)=exp(−u(x)²/2ε²). Sand-grain transport model (real Chladni physics): grains are repelled from antinodes (high |displacement| ⇒ high acceleration tossing grains) and settle where the surface is still, i.e. each particle follows an over-damped drift toward minima of the local vibration energy density E(x)=⟨u(x,t)²⟩ (time-averaged squared displacement): dx_p/dt = −μ ∇E(x_p) + ξ(t), with μ a mobility and ξ small noise to break ties; for a single driven mode E ∝ |w(x,ω)|², so ∇E is analytic. Equivalently a Langevin/gradient-descent on |w|². Color/height: map u or |w| to a colormap or extrude as a 3D height-field (∂z = u) for relief lighting.
techniques.what_it_reveals: Makes the abstract eigenfunction tangible and metrologically honest: particle density becomes a direct estimator of the time-averaged energy field, so the eye reads where the sound deposits vs avoids energy. The convergence speed of grains visualizes damping/Q. Overlaying the analytic nodal contour on the particle pile-up validates that the displayed pattern is the real zero-set, not an artifact.
techniques.why_breathtaking: The granular cascade — tens of thousands of points streaming and self-organizing onto invisible lines in real time — is the single most viscerally satisfying part of cymatics; it reads as matter obeying sound. Combined with a height-field extrusion and rim lighting it becomes a 3D sculpture that breathes with the music.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Particle count and grid resolution auto-scale to the active modal complexity (more/finer nodes ⇒ spawn more grains, refine the marching-squares grid only where |∇u| is large — adaptive subdivision driven by the field itself). The energy field E is recomputed from whatever drives the simulation, so the renderer is agnostic to which physics (plate/membrane/Faraday) produced u.
techniques.implementation_notes: Pure GPU: a wgpu compute shader advances the particle buffer (gradient of E read from the displacement texture via finite differences or analytic derivative), an instanced point/quad pass draws them; marching-squares can run in a compute pass or be approximated by the exp(−u²/2ε²) emissive band in the fragment shader (cheaper, no geometry). 100k+ particles at 60–120 FPS is well within reach (Cymatica ships exactly this on mobile Metal). egui hosts the controls; the heavy lifting is in wgpu. Gotcha: ∇E for the complex driven field is ∇|w|² = 2 Re{ w* ∇w } — keep w complex through the shader. Add slight noise ξ so grains don't freeze on metastable ridges.

references.title: Cymatica — Chladni & cymatics simulator (Metal GPU, 100k sand particles at 60–120 FPS)
Real-Time Sound-Reactive 3D Cymatics (ehsanpo/AudioTools) — particle systems driven by audio frequencies
Chladni Plate Visualisation (UCA research, 2022) — particle/sand simulation approach

key_references.title: Exploring the resonant vibration of thin plates: Reconstruction of Chladni patterns and determination of resonant wave numbers (Tseng, Hsu, et al., J. Acoust. Soc. Am. 137, 2015)
key_references.why: The definitive modern treatment: derives the inhomogeneous Helmholtz / driven response function for an electronically excited plate and shows the steady-state field (not pure eigenmodes) reconstructs real Chladni figures — the exact 'driven response' upgrade the facet asks for.

key_references.title: Resolving the formation of modern Chladni figures (P.-Y. Tseng et al.)
key_references.why: Explains why electronically driven ('modern') Chladni figures differ from classical bowed ones, using Green's-function / resonance-denominator modal sums — the theoretical basis for the audio-driven Lorentzian superposition.

key_references.title: Vibration of a circular membrane — Wikipedia
key_references.why: Clean, complete derivation of the Bessel-function drumhead modes, eigenfrequencies ω_mn = (j_mn/a)·c, and the full standing-wave superposition — directly implementable.

key_references.title: Vibration of plates — Wikipedia
key_references.why: Authoritative source for the Kirchhoff–Love biharmonic equation D∇⁴w+ρhẅ=q, flexural rigidity with Poisson's ratio, boundary conditions, and the simply-supported rectangular closed form.

key_references.why: The math behind Faraday subharmonic pattern selection (stripes/squares/hexagons) via standing-wave amplitude equations — the basis for the most spectacular, emergent visualization class.

key_references.title: The Fundamentals of Modal Testing — App Note 243-3 (HP/Agilent)
key_references.why: Canonical statement of the frequency-response function as a Lorentzian modal sum H(ω)=Σ φᵢφⱼ/((ωₙ²−ω²)+j2ζωₙω) — the exact denominator used to couple audio spectrum to mode amplitudes.

key_references.title: Cymatica — Chladni Plate & Cymatics Simulator
key_references.why: Proof of realtime feasibility: GPU-driven Chladni with 100k sand particles at 60–120 FPS on mobile, validating the wgpu particle-on-nodal-set renderer for the desktop app.

cross_links: FFT/spectral analysis facet: the audio FFT magnitude spectrum A(f) is the direct input vector that drives the Lorentzian modal weights — the cymatics renderer is downstream of whatever spectral analysis the app already runs with rustfft.

Eigen-decomposition / modal-analysis facet (general): the biharmonic and membrane eigenproblems are the same K φ = ω² M φ generalized-eigenvalue machinery used for any modal/PCA/SVD-style spectral visualizer; a shared sparse eigensolver serves both.

Particle systems / GPU compute facet: the sand-grain Langevin transport (dx/dt = −μ∇E + ξ) and marching-squares nodal extraction are a reusable wgpu compute layer shared with any flow-field or gradient-descent visualization.

Pattern-formation / reaction-diffusion facet: the Faraday Swift–Hohenberg surrogate ∂_t u = [ε−(∇²+k_c²)²]u − u³ overlaps directly with reaction-diffusion / Turing-pattern visualizers — same solver, audio drives ε and k_c.

Phase / instantaneous-frequency facet: degenerate membrane modes (cos mθ vs sin mθ) and Faraday traveling waves need a phase input to set rotation direction — stereo phase or analytic-signal instantaneous phase from the audio supplies it.

facet: Color science & perceptual mapping for sound visualization: perceptually-uniform colormaps, magnitude->luminance / frequency->hue, the (non)physics of pitch->color, and how to color a spectrogram so the eye reads it truthfully and gorgeously.
summary: The single most load-bearing fact in this facet is that the human eye reads ORDER almost entirely through luminance, not hue or saturation. A truthful, beautiful spectrogram therefore maps acoustic magnitude (in dB) to a MONOTONICALLY INCREASING perceptual lightness, and uses hue/chroma only as a secondary, decorative or categorical channel. This is exactly why viridis/magma/cividis (designed for constant dJ'/dt in CAM02-UCS) work and why jet/rainbow lie: jet's luminance is non-monotonic (low-high-low), so it manufactures false bright bands at yellow/cyan and hides real structure in its flat green plateau. The pitch->hue rainbow that musicians love is, perceptually, a fiction: Newton's color-music analogy is numerological, and large-N cross-modal studies show hue has essentially NO reliable mapping to any acoustic feature, while pitch and spectral centroid map robustly to LIGHTNESS and loudness maps to SATURATION/chroma. The practical, breathtaking move for a Rust+egui app is: (1) compute color in a cheap perceptual space (Oklab/Oklch — a 3x3 matrix + cube root, trivially realtime), (2) drive lightness L from dB magnitude and reserve hue/chroma for a genuinely orthogonal acoustic variable (instantaneous frequency / phase-derivative, as in NSynth "rainbowgrams"; or per-source identity), and (3) auto-scale the dB window and chroma to the signal so the palette always fills its perceptual range without clipping the sRGB gamut.

techniques.name: Perceptually-uniform sequential colormap (viridis/magma/cividis) via constant lightness slope in CAM02-UCS
techniques.one_line: Map magnitude to a path whose perceptual lightness J' increases at a constant rate, so equal data steps look equal and grayscale-printing still reads correctly.
techniques.the_math: Design in CAM02-UCS (Luo et al. 2006): from CIECAM02 lightness J and colorfulness M, J' = (1+100·c1)·J / (1 + c1·J) with c1=0.007; M' = (1/c2)·ln(1+c2·M) with c2=0.0228; a'=M'·cos(h), b'=M'·sin(h). Perceptual distance ΔE' = sqrt(((ΔJ')/K_L)^2 + (Δa')^2 + (Δb')^2), K_L=1 for the UCS variant. A colormap C(t), t∈[0,1], is 'perceptually uniform' iff the perceptual-delta ||dC/dt||_{CAM02-UCS} is constant (a horizontal line). viridis/magma are built so J'(t) is (nearly) AFFINE in t: dJ'/dt = const. cividis additionally fixes a,b for dichromats by collapsing to a near-1D blue-yellow line and choosing the J'(t) linearization that maximizes J' range.
techniques.what_it_reveals: Reveals true relative magnitude: equal dB steps in the spectrogram produce equal perceived contrast everywhere, so a -40 dB harmonic and a -10 dB fundamental sit at honestly-spaced lightnesses. No false edges, no hidden flat spots. Because lightness is monotonic, it survives grayscale and red-green colorblindness.
techniques.why_breathtaking: It looks like lit volume rather than a painted heatmap — smooth, photographic gradients with a luminous yellow-white core for loud partials, sinking through magenta/purple to near-black silence. The eye glides over it without snagging on artifacts.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: The map itself is fixed, but the MAPPING auto-scales: pick the dB floor as a robust low percentile (e.g. 2nd-percentile of the magnitude histogram, or noise-floor estimate) and the ceiling as the 99.5th percentile, then linearly stretch dB->t so the colormap always spans its full perceptual range for THIS clip. Re-estimate per-frame (with smoothing) for AGC-like adaptation to loud/quiet passages.
techniques.implementation_notes: Trivially realtime in Rust: ship the 256x3 viridis/magma LUT as a const array (matplotlib values are public domain) and index by t. No color-space math at runtime. For GPU, upload the LUT as a 1D texture and sample in the fragment shader. The only per-frame work is the dB stretch + histogram percentile (O(bins)). This is the safe default and should be the app's baseline spectrogram palette.

references.title: matplotlib colormaps (Smith & van der Walt, viridis design talk)
Nuñez, Anderton, Renslow — cividis / Optimizing colormaps for CVD (PLOS ONE)
CIECAM02 / CAM02-UCS (Wikipedia)

techniques.name: Why jet/rainbow lies — non-monotonic luminance and false banding
techniques.the_math: For jet, parameterize luminance Y(t) ≈ 0.2126 R + 0.7152 G + 0.0722 B (Rec.709 linear) along t∈[0,1]: Y(t) is non-monotonic, rising from blue (~0.07) to a peak around yellow/green then falling to red (~0.21). Its derivative dY/dt changes sign, so iso-luminant pairs occur (blue≈red), and |dY/dt| spikes near the yellow and cyan transitions. In CAM02-UCS the perceptual-delta ||dC/dt|| is far from constant: large near cyan/yellow (sharp false edges) and near-zero across the green plateau (a perceptual 'flat spot' that can hide ~1/10 of the data range, per Kovesi). Crucially CIELAB/CAM02 uniformity holds only at LOW spatial frequency; at the ~spectrogram detail scale (several cycles/degree) chromatic acuity collapses and ONLY the luminance channel carries fine structure — so rainbow's hue-encoded detail is literally invisible at the pixel scale.
techniques.one_line: Rainbow maps encode order in hue, which the eye can't rank and can't resolve at high spatial frequency — manufacturing edges that aren't there and erasing edges that are.
techniques.what_it_reveals: Diagnostically: it reveals what NOT to do. The flat green plateau hides graded structure; the bright yellow band invents a contour. Physicians using jet for cardiac maps are measurably slower and more error-prone. For a spectrogram it means harmonics in the green range merge while a meaningless yellow ridge pops.
techniques.why_breathtaking: Anti-pattern. Its garish saturation reads as 'busy' and 'scientific' to laypeople but is perceptually dishonest. Useful as an in-app A/B toggle to SHOW the user the lie.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: None — its pathology is fixed and content-independent, which is itself the point: it cannot adapt to reveal anything.
techniques.implementation_notes: Cheap to render (LUT) but should be offered only as a deliberate 'legacy/contrast' option with a tooltip. If high-frequency-detail contrast is genuinely wanted, use turbo instead (C2-continuous, CVD-safe for 99.997%) — same vivid look, no banding.

references.title: Kovesi — Good Colour Maps: How to Design Them (arXiv:1509.03700)
Turbo, an improved rainbow colormap (Google Research)
A dangerous rainbow: why colormaps matter (MathWorks)

techniques.name: Oklab/Oklch runtime perceptual mapping — magnitude->lightness, orthogonal variable->hue/chroma
techniques.one_line: Build spectrogram color on the fly in Oklab: drive L from dB, h/C from a second acoustic feature, so the eye reads magnitude as brightness and the feature as color without lying.
techniques.the_math: Forward (linear sRGB -> Oklab): l,m,s = M1·(R,G,B) with M1=[[0.4122214708,0.5363325363,0.0514459929],[0.2119034982,0.6806995451,0.1073969566],[0.0883024619,0.2817188376,0.6299787005]]; l'=cbrt(l), m'=cbrt(m), s'=cbrt(s); (L,a,b)=M2·(l',m',s') with M2=[[0.2104542553,0.7936177850,-0.0040720468],[1.9779984951,-2.4285922050,0.4505937099],[0.0259040371,0.7827717662,-0.8086757660]]. Oklch: C=sqrt(a^2+b^2), h=atan2(b,a); inverse a=C·cos h, b=C·sin h, then invert M2, cube each, invert M1. To COLOR: set L = L_min + (L_max−L_min)·g(dB_norm) (g a mild gamma), h = feature_hue, C = C_target·sat_norm, then convert Oklch->sRGB and gamut-clip. Cube root (γ=1/3) is the cheap perceptual lightness compressor.
techniques.what_it_reveals: Lets you show TWO orthogonal acoustic dimensions truthfully at once: lightness = energy (the dimension the eye ranks), hue = an independent variable (source ID, harmonic vs noise, instantaneous frequency). Because Oklab fixes CIELAB's blue-hue twist (Ebner-Fairchild RMS 0.49 vs 0.69), equal hue steps look equal — a smooth phase->hue sweep won't kink.
techniques.why_breathtaking: You get the luminous photographic gradient of viridis PLUS controllable, perceptually-even color — e.g. an iso-luminant hue wheel for phase that shimmers like an oil-slick while energy still reads as glow. Full artistic control of the palette without sacrificing honesty.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Strong: L_min/L_max set from the dB percentile window (AGC); C_target auto-reduced toward sRGB-gamut-safe maxima per (L,h) so saturated passages never clip; hue range can auto-rotate to maximize separation of the K active sources detected in the frame. Everything self-fits the clip.
techniques.implementation_notes: Two 3x3 matmuls + 3 cube roots + 3 powers per pixel — fine on CPU for a strip, ideal on wgpu/glow fragment shader for full-res realtime. No external crate needed (palette crate exists but rolling the 6 constants is simpler). Gamut clip: reduce C at fixed L,h until in [0,1]^3 (Ottosson's method) or just clamp+desaturate. Watch: cbrt of negative LMS shouldn't occur for in-gamut inputs but guard anyway.

references.title: Ottosson — A perceptual color space for image processing (Oklab)
Ottosson — sRGB gamut clipping
Oklab color space (Wikipedia)

techniques.name: Rainbowgram — magnitude->intensity, instantaneous-frequency (phase derivative)->hue
techniques.one_line: Encode CQT/STFT magnitude as luminance and the time-derivative of unwrapped phase (instantaneous frequency) as hue, exposing timbral structure invisible in a plain magnitude spectrogram.
techniques.the_math: From STFT X(t,f)=|X|e^{iφ(t,f)}: magnitude channel mag = 20·log10(|X|) -> L (or alpha/intensity). Instantaneous frequency IF(t,f) = (1/2π)·dφ/dt, computed as the per-bin phase advance between hops minus the expected advance: Δφ = wrap(φ(t,f) − φ(t−1,f) − 2π·f·H/Fs) where H=hop, wrapped to (−π,π]; IF = f + Δφ·Fs/(2π·H). Map (IF − f_bin) or normalized IF to HUE via a CYCLIC colormap (hue is angular, matching phase's S^1 topology): hue = (IF mod Δf)/Δf · 360°. A harmonic exactly on-bin shows a single steady hue line; drift/vibrato sweeps hue -> rainbows.
techniques.what_it_reveals: Two magnitude spectrograms that look identical can sound completely different because of phase; the rainbowgram makes that phase/IF structure visible as color. Reveals fine pitch drift, vibrato, inharmonicity, and partial-locking far below the FFT bin resolution — structure you literally cannot get from |X| alone.
techniques.why_breathtaking: It is the canonical 'gorgeous AND informative' audio image: glowing harmonic filaments tinted by their micro-frequency, blooming into literal rainbows on vibrato. The name is earned.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Hue scale auto-fits: normalize IF deviation by local bin width so the cyclic hue range always spans the observed drift; magnitude->L uses the same per-clip dB AGC window as the sequential map. CQT auto-chooses log-spaced bins matching musical pitch, self-scaling the frequency axis to the content's register.
techniques.implementation_notes: rustfft gives X; phase derivative is one subtract + wrap per bin per hop (cheap). Needs a CYCLIC perceptual colormap for hue (Kovesi C2/C4, or an iso-luminant Oklch hue wheel) so the wrap point isn't a false seam. CQT is heavier than STFT but a log-bin reassignment of an STFT is a good realtime approximation. Use Oklch with FIXED L and only h varying to keep magnitude readable on the intensity channel.

references.title: Engel et al. — NSynth: Neural Audio Synthesis (rainbowgrams)

Engel et al. — Neural Audio Synthesis of Musical Notes (arXiv:1704.01279)

techniques.name: The (non)physics of pitch->hue: cross-modal correspondences, not Newton's analogy
techniques.one_line: There is no perceptually valid pitch->hue law; the robust, replicable couplings are pitch/centroid->LIGHTNESS and loudness->SATURATION, with hue essentially unmapped.
techniques.the_math: Not a transform but a mapping policy backed by data. Cross-modal magnitude alignment: perceived 'magnitude' rank is shared across loudness, size, and (inverse) pitch — higher pitch ~ smaller/lighter/brighter. Quantitatively (Anikin/large-N implicit-association studies): high pitch -> lighter (≈3–4% fewer errors, ≈64 ms faster RT), high spectral centroid -> lighter (≈4%, ≈83 ms), loud -> more saturated (≈4%, ≈84 ms), high pitch -> more saturated (≈5%). HUE shows 'nearly complete lack of association' with any acoustic contrast (one marginal high-pitch->blue, ~1%). Newton's 7-color/diatonic mapping is numerology (he forced indigo to make 7 match the scale); the visible spectrum spans <1 octave of light frequency, so any 'octave=hue' scheme is an arbitrary convention. Genuine chromesthetes ARE internally consistent (test-retest) but their hue choices are idiosyncratic and disagree across individuals — confirming hue carries no universal signal, while their LIGHTNESS (higher note = lighter) is shared.
techniques.what_it_reveals: Reveals the design constraint, not the sound: tells you to spend your reliable perceptual budget (lightness, then saturation) on the dimensions humans actually share, and treat any pitch/note->hue palette as decorative convention, not perception. Prevents building a 'scientific' rainbow that no two users read the same way.
techniques.why_breathtaking: Indirectly enabling: a palette that respects this (energy/pitch -> glow, loudness -> vividness) feels intuitively 'right' to nearly everyone, including non-synesthetes, because it rides the universal correspondences. Optional per-user note->hue assignment can still delight individual synesthetes.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Lets the app pick a default that is content- and user-robust: drive L by pitch/centroid and C by loudness automatically; expose a user-editable note->hue ring for those who want their personal chromesthetic palette (store per-user, since it's idiosyncratic).
techniques.implementation_notes: No DSP cost — it's a policy that shapes the Oklab mapping above. Implementation = choose which acoustic feature feeds L vs C vs h. The science says: NEVER make hue the sole carrier of pitch order if truthful reading matters; if you offer a note->hue wheel, make it user-remappable and iso-luminant so it doesn't fight the magnitude channel.

references.title: Anikin & Johansson — Implicit associations between individual properties of color and sound (PMC6407832)
Spence & Di Stefano — Coloured hearing, colour music, colour organs (i-Perception 2022)
Standardized synesthesia test battery (Eagleman et al., PMC4118597)

techniques.name: Cyclic perceptually-uniform colormaps for phase / pitch-class (the circle-of-fifths topology)
techniques.one_line: Phase and pitch-class live on a circle, so color them with a cyclic map of constant lightness whose endpoints meet seamlessly — no false seam, no luminance ramp masquerading as order.
techniques.the_math: A cyclic map C(t), t∈[0,1), must satisfy C(0)=C(1) AND match derivatives at the wrap. Kovesi builds them as zig-zag or 'diamond' paths in CIELAB with two equal-lightness extrema so the four reference colors are perceptually equidistant; lightness reversals are Gaussian-smoothed to kill false edges. The clean modern form is an ISO-LUMINANT Oklch ring: fix L=L0, C=C0, sweep h=2π·t -> (a,b)=(C0 cos2πt, C0 sin2πt) -> sRGB. Perceptual uniformity along the ring requires |dC/dt|_perceptual = const, i.e. equal Oklch hue steps (Oklab's hue linearity makes this near-automatic, unlike HSV whose green/blue arcs are visually uneven). Use for STFT phase φ∈(−π,π] (hue=φ) or pitch-class p∈{0..11} (hue=2π p/12).
techniques.what_it_reveals: Shows angular/periodic structure honestly: phase wrap-around, pitch-class identity across octaves (chroma feature / chromagram), circle-of-fifths motion. A linear map on a circular quantity would put a hard fake discontinuity at the wrap; the cyclic map removes it so genuine continuity reads as continuous.
techniques.why_breathtaking: An iso-luminant hue ring shimmers — pure chroma rotation at constant brightness gives a satiny, neon-on-velvet look, perfect for a phase field or a rotating chromagram, while never stealing brightness from a magnitude overlay.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Self-fits the period to the data's natural cycle: 2π for phase, 12 for pitch-class, N for an N-fold symmetry detected via autocorrelation. C0 auto-set to the gamut-safe max chroma at L0 so the ring is as vivid as sRGB allows without clipping.
techniques.implementation_notes: Cheap: precompute a 256-entry cyclic LUT (Kovesi's CET-C maps are published, or generate the Oklch ring once at startup). Realtime sampling is a single texture lookup. Key correctness point: the LUT MUST be C0/C1 continuous at index 0==255 or you reintroduce a seam. Pair with the rainbowgram (phase->hue) or a chromagram tab.

references.title: Kovesi — Good Colour Maps: cyclic map design (arXiv:1509.03700, §cyclic)
CET cyclic perceptual colour maps (cetcolor vignette)
Oklab color space — hue linearity (Wikipedia)

techniques.name: HSLuv / CIELUV-saturation mapping for honest, gamut-aware categorical source coloring
techniques.one_line: When coloring multiple sources/stems by identity, use HSLuv so equal-lightness picks actually look equally bright and saturation is a true percentage of achievable chroma — clean, balanced, never accidentally garish.
techniques.the_math: HSLuv = CIELCh(uv) with chroma replaced by S = C / C_max(L,h)·100, where C_max(L,h) is the maximum chroma the sRGB gamut allows at that lightness and hue (found by intersecting the constant-(L,h) ray with the gamut hull). CIELUV: from XYZ, u'=4X/(X+15Y+3Z), v'=9Y/(X+15Y+3Z); L*=116 f(Y/Yn)−16 with f(t)=t^{1/3} if t>(6/29)^3 else (1/3)(29/6)^2 t + 4/29; u*=13L*(u'−u'n), v*=13L*(v'−v'n); C=sqrt(u*^2+v*^2), h=atan2(v*,u*). Choosing N maximally-distinct source colors = N hues equally spaced on the HSLuv ring at fixed L,S (a perceptually even qualitative palette).
techniques.what_it_reveals: Reveals source SEPARATION without implying false order: distinct stems/partials get equally-salient, equally-bright colors so none spuriously dominates. Because S is normalized to the local gamut, no hue is accidentally duller or louder than another — the eye reads 'different category', not 'bigger value'.
techniques.why_breathtaking: Produces those crisp, designer-grade multi-track palettes (think a balanced equalizer rainbow where every band looks intentional). Equal-lightness guarantees a calm, non-vibrating set even at high saturation.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Auto-generates an N-color qualitative palette for however many sources/lanes are active: hues at 360°·k/N, L and S chosen to maximize min pairwise Oklab ΔE while staying in gamut. Re-solves when track count changes. Can also auto-pick L to maximize contrast against the current background theme (dark/light).
techniques.implementation_notes: HSLuv has a tiny public-domain Rust crate (hsluv) or ~150 lines (CIELUV + per-(L,h) gamut intersection via the sRGB cubic boundary). Forward/inverse is matmul + cube root + one root-find for C_max — fine at palette-build time (not per-pixel). Use for lane colors, stem overlays, EQ bands; not for the continuous magnitude field (use viridis/Oklab there). Oklch's gamut intersection (Ottosson) is an equally good, arguably simpler modern substitute.

cross_links: Spectrogram & STFT facet: the dB-magnitude field and instantaneous-frequency / phase-derivative that this facet COLORS come from the STFT/CQT pipeline; the rainbowgram needs the phase channel, not just |X|.

Autonomous dimensioning facet: the per-clip dB-percentile AGC window, gamut-safe chroma auto-reduction, and N-color qualitative palette generation are concrete self-scaling mechanisms shared with axis/range auto-fitting.
Constant-Q / log-frequency & chromagram facet: cyclic perceptual maps are the correct coloring for the circular pitch-class (chroma) and circle-of-fifths topology.

Loudness / psychoacoustics facet: mapping magnitude to perceptual LIGHTNESS parallels mapping amplitude to perceived LOUDNESS (dB, A-weighting); both are about matching a physical scale to a perceptual one before display.
GPU/wgpu rendering facet: Oklab/Oklch and cyclic-LUT sampling belong in the fragment shader for realtime full-resolution coloring; LUT-as-1D-texture is the shared mechanism.

facet: Dimensionality reduction & embeddings — the autonomous-dimensioning star
summary: Dimensionality reduction (DR) is the purest expression of "autonomous dimensioning": you compute a high-dimensional feature trajectory of a sound (MFCC, chroma, spectral descriptors, or a neural latent), and the algorithm itself discovers the 2D/3D axes that best preserve the structure — no human chooses what frequency, what timbre dimension, what cluster boundary matters. PCA finds the orthogonal directions of maximum variance in closed form (eigendecomposition of the covariance), and its eigenvectors ARE interpretable spectral axes; t-SNE and UMAP are nonlinear neighbor-embedding methods that unfold the perceptual manifold so that timbrally-similar grains land together, revealing cluster structure (kicks vs hats, vowels vs fricatives) invisible in a spectrogram; SOMs lay a topology-preserving grid over timbre space that doubles as a navigable 2D "map of the sound"; and audio autoencoders/VAEs (RAVE) learn a compact latent whose axes are smooth morphable timbre controls. The four methods trade off cleanly: PCA and Oja's-rule streaming PCA are trivially realtime in Rust+rustfft and self-scale via eigenvalue ratios; SOM is a cheap online grid update; t-SNE/UMAP are the breathtaking ones but need GPU or precompute, with parametric/progressive variants and a learned projection function for streaming. The deepest autonomous-dimensioning idea here is using the eigenvalue spectrum (PCA) or trustworthiness/continuity metrics to let the SOUND decide how many dimensions it actually occupies, and to auto-orient/auto-scale the embedding axes frame-to-frame.

techniques.name: PCA on a sliding feature window (streaming eigen-axes of timbre)
techniques.one_line: Eigendecompose the running covariance of MFCC/spectral feature vectors; the top eigenvectors are the sound's own self-discovered axes, the eigenvalue spectrum tells you how many dimensions it truly occupies.
techniques.the_math: Feature matrix X ∈ R^{N×d} (N frames, d features e.g. 13 MFCC + deltas). Center: X̃ = X − μ, μ = (1/N)Σ x_i. Covariance C = (1/(N−1)) X̃ᵀX̃ ∈ R^{d×d}. Eigendecomposition C = VΛVᵀ, eigenvalues λ_1≥λ_2≥… , eigenvectors v_k. Projection onto top-2: y_i = [v_1·x̃_i, v_2·x̃_i]. Variance explained by k dims: ρ_k = (Σ_{j≤k} λ_j)/(Σ_j λ_j) — this IS the autonomous-dimensioning knob: pick smallest k with ρ_k ≥ 0.95. Streaming via Oja's rule (no covariance stored): wₜ = wₜ₋₁ + ηₜ yₜ(xₜ − yₜ wₜ₋₁), yₜ = wₜ₋₁·xₜ, which converges w → top eigenvector; deflate x ← x − (w·x)w for component 2,3,…
techniques.what_it_reveals: The dominant axes of timbral variation the sound actually uses — e.g. PC1 often correlates with spectral centroid/brightness, PC2 with spectral spread or harmonic-vs-noise. The eigenvalue spectrum reveals intrinsic dimensionality: a pure tone collapses to ~1 dim (λ_1≫rest), a rich orchestral mix spreads across many. You cannot hear 'this passage lives on a 2D manifold' but PCA shows it.
techniques.why_breathtaking: Watching the principal axes rotate in realtime as the music's timbre shifts — the coordinate frame literally chasing the sound — is hypnotic, and a 3D PCA point-cloud of a whole track traces a luminous trajectory through timbre space.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Maximal: axes are eigenvectors (chosen by data, not human), scale is set by √λ_k (each axis auto-normalized to its variance), and the number of meaningful dimensions is read directly off the eigenvalue decay (ρ_k threshold or the spectral gap λ_k/λ_{k+1}).
techniques.implementation_notes: Trivial in Rust: nalgebra/ndarray-linalg SymmetricEigen on a d×d (d≤~40) matrix is microseconds. Streaming Oja's rule is a handful of FLOPs per frame, zero allocation, perfect for the audio thread. Gotcha: eigenvectors have sign ambiguity (v and −v equivalent) and can swap order when λ_k≈λ_{k+1} — stabilize with sign-pinning (force max-abs component positive) and temporal smoothing/Procrustes alignment to the previous frame's basis to avoid axis-flip flicker.

references.title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA): Explained Step-by-Step | Built In
Oja learning rule — Scholarpedia
incrementalPCA — Incremental principal component analysis (MATLAB docs)

techniques.name: UMAP of audio-grain feature vectors (fuzzy-simplicial manifold of timbre)
techniques.one_line: Build a fuzzy k-NN graph in MFCC/STFT space, then optimize a 2D layout by cross-entropy so neighbors attract and non-neighbors repel — UMAP discovers the perceptual clusters of a sound corpus far faster and with better local+global structure than t-SNE.
techniques.the_math: High-dim membership: for point x_i with k nearest neighbors, ρ_i = dist to nearest neighbor (local connectivity), and σ_i solved so Σ_j exp(−max(0, d(x_i,x_j)−ρ_i)/σ_i) = log₂(k). Directed weight w_{i|j} = exp(−max(0, d(x_i,x_j)−ρ_i)/σ_i). Symmetrize (probabilistic t-conorm): w_{ij} = w_{i|j} + w_{j|i} − w_{i|j}·w_{j|i}. Low-dim kernel: q_{ij} = 1/(1 + a‖y_i−y_j‖^{2b}), with a,b least-squares fit to the desired min_dist curve (≈ a=1.93, b=0.79 for min_dist=0.1). Optimize cross-entropy CE = Σ [w_{ij} log(w_{ij}/q_{ij}) + (1−w_{ij}) log((1−w_{ij})/(1−q_{ij}))] by SGD: attractive grad ∝ (−2ab d^{2(b−1)})/(1+a d^{2b}) · w_{ij}(y_i−y_j); repulsive (negative sampling) grad ∝ (2b)/((ε+d²)(1+a d^{2b})) · (1−w_{ij})(y_i−y_j).
techniques.what_it_reveals: The cluster topology of a sample library or a track's grains: percussive hits separate from sustained tones, hi-hats stratify away from kicks by frequency content, vowels form a smooth ring while fricatives clump — manifold structure no spectrogram exposes. n_neighbors trades local vs global structure (5≈local timbre cliques, 50≈global genre layout).
techniques.why_breathtaking: The signature UMAP 'galaxy' — thousands of glowing grain-points self-organizing into filaments and islands of timbre — is one of the most striking sound visualizations possible; coloring by time or pitch turns a track into a living constellation.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: σ_i is solved per-point so every grain gets its own adaptive bandwidth (dense regions tighten, sparse regions widen) — the embedding self-calibrates local scale; a,b auto-fit from min_dist; n_neighbors is the only true human knob and can itself be set from corpus size (≈√N).
techniques.implementation_notes: Hardest realtime target. Full UMAP is batch (k-NN graph + many SGD epochs); for a fixed library precompute offline and stream points in. For realtime, use Parametric UMAP (a trained encoder net gives O(1) out-of-sample projection — a forward pass per new frame) or Progressive UMAP for streaming inserts. k-NN is the bottleneck; use HNSW (hnsw_rs crate). The SGD layout maps cleanly to a wgpu compute shader (per-edge attraction + negative sampling). Realistic Rust path: offline UMAP via a Python sidecar or a from-scratch port for static corpora; parametric encoder (tiny MLP via candle/burn) for live.

references.title: UMAP and its Variants: Tutorial and Survey (arXiv:2109.02508)
lmcinnes/umap — reference implementation
Comparative Audio Analysis with Wavenet, MFCCs, UMAP, t-SNE and PCA — Leon Fedden

techniques.name: t-SNE of spectral/MFCC features (perplexity-tuned timbre clusters)
techniques.one_line: Model pairwise neighbor probabilities with Gaussians in feature space and heavy-tailed Student-t in 2D, then minimize KL divergence — t-SNE produces the classic well-separated clusters of similar sounds.
techniques.the_math: High-dim conditional: p_{j|i} = exp(−‖x_i−x_j‖²/2σ_i²) / Σ_{k≠i} exp(−‖x_i−x_k‖²/2σ_i²), p_{i|i}=0. σ_i chosen by binary search so perplexity Perp(P_i)=2^{H(P_i)} matches target (5–50), H(P_i)=−Σ_j p_{j|i}log₂ p_{j|i}. Symmetric joint: p_{ij}=(p_{j|i}+p_{i|j})/(2N). Low-dim Student-t (1 dof): q_{ij}=(1+‖y_i−y_j‖²)^{-1} / Σ_{k≠l}(1+‖y_k−y_l‖²)^{-1}. Cost C = KL(P‖Q)=Σ_{i≠j} p_{ij} log(p_{ij}/q_{ij}). Gradient: ∂C/∂y_i = 4 Σ_j (p_{ij}−q_{ij})(y_i−y_j)(1+‖y_i−y_j‖²)^{-1}, optimized by momentum gradient descent.
techniques.what_it_reveals: Sharp perceptual clusters — instrument classes, phoneme groups, drum types — emerge as visually distinct islands. Reveals that the sound's feature space is clumpy, not continuous, in a way you cannot hear. The Student-t heavy tail fixes the 'crowding problem,' giving the wide whitespace between clusters that makes structure legible.
techniques.why_breathtaking: The crisp, well-separated archipelago of clusters is iconic and immediately readable; animating the gradient-descent 'unfolding' from random init to settled clusters is mesmerizing.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Per-point σ_i is auto-solved to hit a fixed perplexity — each point adapts its Gaussian bandwidth to local density (the core self-scaling mechanism). Perplexity sets the effective neighbor count and can be auto-derived from N. But t-SNE warps global distances, so axes carry no absolute meaning (no auto-scale of the plane itself).
techniques.implementation_notes: Not realtime-friendly: O(N²) naive (Barnes-Hut/FFT-accelerated tree code gets O(N log N)), non-parametric (every new frame needs re-optimization → no clean streaming), non-convex (random restarts give different maps → temporally unstable for live audio). Best used for offline static visualization of a sample library or a frozen track. Klustr reported ~45 min for 10k samples vs UMAP's ~2 min — UMAP is the better realtime bet. In Rust: bhtsne crate exists; reserve for precompute, not the audio thread.

t-SNE — Laurens van der Maaten (original code & papers)
Explainable Instrument Classification: MFCC + t-SNE + Grad-CAM (MDPI Information)

techniques.name: Self-Organizing Map of timbre (topology-preserving navigable sound grid)
techniques.one_line: Train a 2D grid of weight vectors so each cell becomes a prototype timbre and neighboring cells stay similar — the result is a literal map you can walk through, and it updates online frame-by-frame.
techniques.the_math: Grid of nodes v with weight w_v ∈ R^d (d = feature dim) at lattice positions r_v. For input x(t): BMU u = argmin_v ‖x(t) − w_v‖. Update all nodes: w_v(s+1) = w_v(s) + θ(u,v,s)·α(s)·(x(t) − w_v(s)). Gaussian neighborhood: θ(u,v,s) = exp(−‖r_u − r_v‖² / (2σ(s)²)). Decays: learning rate α(s) = α_0 exp(−s/λ_α) (or 1−s/λ), neighborhood radius σ(s) = σ_0 exp(−s/λ_σ). U-matrix for visualization: U(v) = average ‖w_v − w_{v'}‖ over grid-neighbors v' — high U = cluster boundary (drawn as ridges/dark walls between timbre regions).
techniques.what_it_reveals: A topographic 'Islands of Music' map: smooth regions of similar timbre with ridge-like boundaries (U-matrix) between dissimilar zones; component planes show how each individual feature (brightness, roughness, percussiveness) varies across the map. Reveals the global continuity/discontinuity structure of timbre space and lets you SEE where a sound sits relative to all others.
techniques.why_breathtaking: The U-matrix relief map — glowing valleys of similar sound separated by dark mountain ridges — looks like an alien topographic map of the audio, and a live cursor (the BMU) hopping across it as the track plays is gorgeous and intuitive.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Strong: the map self-organizes its layout (which timbres go where is emergent, not assigned); σ(s) shrinks automatically from global to local ordering; the U-matrix auto-discovers cluster boundaries and intrinsic structure. Grid size is the main human choice (heuristic ≈ 5√N nodes).
techniques.implementation_notes: Excellent realtime fit. A 30×30 grid of d≈40 vectors = 900 distance computations per frame, fully parallelizable; pure CPU is fine, or a wgpu compute pass for big grids. Genuinely online (each frame nudges the map) → naturally streaming, unlike t-SNE. No external linalg needed. Gotchas: BMU search is the cost (use SIMD); decay schedules need tuning so the live map neither freezes nor thrashes; render U-matrix as a heightmap mesh in egui/wgpu. Batch SOM variant gives more stability if live jitter is a problem.

techniques.name: Audio autoencoder / VAE latent trajectory (learned timbre coordinates)
techniques.one_line: A neural encoder compresses each audio frame into a smooth low-dim latent whose axes are continuous, morphable timbre controls; plotting the latent trajectory shows the sound moving through a learned perceptual space.
techniques.the_math: VAE encoder q_φ(z|x) outputs μ(x), σ(x); sample z = μ + σ⊙ε, ε~N(0,I). Loss = reconstruction + β·KL: L = E_{q_φ}[log p_θ(x|z)] − β·D_KL(q_φ(z|x)‖N(0,I)), with D_KL = ½ Σ_k (μ_k² + σ_k² − log σ_k² − 1). The KL term forces an axis-aligned, disentangled latent so each dimension is an independent timbre factor. RAVE adds adversarial fine-tuning and runs encoder in realtime (20× faster than realtime, <10 ms latency, 48 kHz). For 2D display, plot the top-2 latent dims directly, or PCA/UMAP the latent if dim>3. Latent variance per dim (or KL contribution per dim) ranks which axes are 'active' — most VAE dims collapse to the prior (KL≈0) and only a few carry information.
techniques.what_it_reveals: The disentangled generative factors of the timbre: smooth interpolation along one latent axis sweeps brightness, another sweeps attack/noisiness — structure that is learned, not hand-designed. The trajectory through latent space reveals how a performance moves between timbral 'concepts,' and you can resynthesize from any point (the embedding is invertible, unlike t-SNE/UMAP).
techniques.why_breathtaking: A point gliding through a learned latent manifold that you can also HEAR (resynthesize as you move) closes the loop between vision and sound — morphing between timbres along a glowing path is uniquely compelling.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: The strongest form: the β-KL pressure makes the network automatically allocate dimensions, pruning unused axes (posterior collapse) so the latent's effective dimensionality is learned from the data; per-dim KL tells you exactly how many axes the timbre needs. Axes are the network's own discovered factors.
techniques.implementation_notes: Inference is realtime-feasible — RAVE's encoder runs faster-than-realtime on a laptop CPU; in Rust load a pretrained model via candle/burn or an ONNX runtime (ort/tract) and run encode() per audio block. Training is offline and GPU-heavy (out of scope for the app; ship a pretrained encoder). Gotchas: model size and per-block latency budget; latent axes can rotate/permute between models so labels aren't fixed; needs a bundled checkpoint. Highest insight-per-pixel but the heaviest dependency.

references.title: RAVE: A variational autoencoder for fast and high-quality neural audio synthesis (arXiv:2111.05011)
Conditioning Autoencoder Latent Spaces for Real-Time Timbre Interpolation and Synthesis (arXiv:2001.11296)
RAVE project page — ACIDS IRCAM

techniques.name: Streaming projection with trustworthiness/continuity-gated dimensionality
techniques.one_line: Continuously project the live feature trajectory with a fast learned/linear map AND monitor embedding-quality metrics to auto-decide 2D vs 3D and when to recompute the basis.
techniques.the_math: Out-of-sample projection function f: R^d→R^2 (parametric UMAP encoder, learned linear map, or fixed PCA basis) gives y_t = f(x_t) per frame in O(1). Quality metrics computed on a sliding window: Trustworthiness T(k) = 1 − (2/(Nk(2N−3k−1))) Σ_i Σ_{j∈U_i^{(k)}} (r(i,j)−k), where U_i^{(k)} are points that are k-NN in the low-dim map but NOT in high-dim, r(i,j) = high-dim rank — penalizes false neighbors. Continuity is the dual (penalizes missed neighbors). Autonomous rule: if T_2D < τ (e.g. 0.9), promote to 3D; recompute/refit basis when drift Σ‖x_t − reconstruct(x_t)‖² (PCA residual) exceeds a threshold → the sound itself triggers re-dimensioning. Streaming techniques: Progressive UMAP for online inserts; Xtreaming for incremental multidimensional projection.
techniques.what_it_reveals: Whether the current 2D view is faithful or lying — and exposes when a sound has become complex enough to need a third axis. Makes the visualization honest: it shows its own distortion and adapts resolution/dimensionality to the live content instead of forcing a fixed map.
techniques.why_breathtaking: A view that fluidly grows from a 2D plane into a 3D cloud exactly when the music gets timbrally richer — the dimensionality of the picture breathing with the sound — is a rare and elegant effect.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: This IS the facet's thesis made literal: trustworthiness/continuity and PCA reconstruction error drive automatic choices of dimension count, axis recompute timing, and resolution. The number of displayed dimensions is a function of measured embedding fidelity, fully data-driven.
techniques.implementation_notes: Feasible as an orchestration layer over PCA (cheap exact streaming basis) plus a parametric encoder for nonlinear cases. Trustworthiness over a windowed k-NN is O(N·k) per update — fine for windows of a few hundred frames with an HNSW index. Gotchas: animating 2D→3D transitions smoothly (lerp a z-axis in/out), hysteresis on the τ thresholds to avoid flicker, and keeping the parametric encoder's training distribution matched to live input. Best built on the PCA streaming core (technique 1) with metrics as the controller.

key_references.why: Authoritative derivation of UMAP's fuzzy-simplicial-set math, the 1/(1+a d^2b) kernel, cross-entropy optimization, and a catalogue of variants (parametric, progressive, densMAP) that matter for realtime/streaming audio.
