key_references.why: Feasibility cornerstone: ort/tract give forward-only realtime inference (latent walks, vocoders, activation viz) while candle/burn add the autograd needed for saliency, integrated gradients, and feature inversion -- the runtime layer deciding what is shippable in Rust+egui+wgpu.

key_references.title: DDSP: Differentiable Digital Signal Processing (Engel et al., ICLR 2020) -- arXiv 2001.04643
key_references.why: Source of the exact harmonic+filtered-noise synthesis equations, modified-sigmoid amplitude nonlinearity, and multi-scale spectral loss that define the interpretable control-surface visual.

cross_links: Dimensionality-reduction facet: RAVE/autoencoder latents are the same embeddings -- here they are decoded back to audio (generative), there they are scattered as points (analytic); PCA-on-latent (GANSpace) is the bridge.
Spectrogram / STFT facet: every technique here renders onto or reconstructs a spectrogram; Griffin-Lim phase recovery and the multi-scale spectral loss reuse that facet's STFT machinery.
Pitch / f0 & harmonic-analysis facet: DDSP's control surface is f0 + loudness + harmonic distribution -- the non-neural version is built directly from a pitch tracker plus harmonic-bin sampling.
Saliency/attention overlays connect to any classifier-driven facet (genre, instrument, event detection): the saliency map explains whatever model that facet trains.
Phase-recovery / vocoder facet: feature inversion and activation-maximization both need spectrogram-to-waveform resynthesis (Griffin-Lim or neural vocoder) to become audible.

facet: Hyperbolic / non-Euclidean & graph-network layouts for harmonic and timbral hierarchy
summary: Hierarchical music structure — Schenkerian/GTTM reductions, tonal-hierarchy nesting, song-form trees, chord-transition graphs, grain/section similarity networks — is exponentially branching, and that is exactly the regime where negatively-curved (hyperbolic) space embeds with far lower distortion than Euclidean. The Poincaré disk gives a circumference/area that grow as 2π·sinh(r) / 2π·(cosh(r)−1), an "impedance match" to a tree whose node count grows exponentially with depth, so a whole harmonic or formal hierarchy fits in one disk with a fisheye focus+context view and literally infinite zoom-by-Möbius-isometry. The strongest techniques here are (1) the Lamping–Rao hyperbolic browser for a focus+context song-form/harmony tree navigated by Möbius transformations, (2) Sarkar/Poincaré combinatorial or gradient embedding of a similarity/harmony tree into the disk with an auto-tuned scaling factor τ, and (3) Euclidean force-directed layouts (Fruchterman–Reingold / spring-electrical) of chord-transition Markov networks and self-similarity section graphs, which reveal pivot chords (centrality), tonal regions (community detection) and small-world topology invisible to the ear. Autonomous dimensioning is unusually natural here: τ self-scales to the tree's depth and max degree, force-layout's optimal length k=C·√(area/|V|) self-scales to node count, and δ-hyperbolicity of the data tells you how much it even wants to be hyperbolic. All are realtime-feasible in Rust+egui: the layouts are O(n) or O(n log n), and the only GPU-worthy part is drawing geodesic arcs and thousands of edges.

techniques.name: Lamping–Rao hyperbolic browser for song-form / harmony tree (focus+context, Möbius-navigated)
techniques.one_line: Lay the formal/harmonic tree on the hyperbolic plane, map to the Poincaré disk for a fisheye 'whole structure at a glance + infinite zoom' view, and navigate by Möbius isometries.
techniques.the_math: Poincaré disk metric ds² = 4(dx²+dy²)/(1−|z|²)². Distance d(u,v)=arcosh(1 + 2|u−v|²/((1−|u|²)(1−|v|²))). Recursive layout: each node is given an angular wedge; children are placed at a FIXED hyperbolic distance s from the parent (in the disk this is a Euclidean step that shrinks toward the rim), and a node owning a wedge of half-angle θ subdivides it among its k children so each child's subtree fits — the exponential room means deep subtrees never crowd. Geodesics are circular arcs orthogonal to the unit circle. Change of focus to point p is the Möbius isometry of the disk written in complex form z ↦ e^{iα}(z − p)/(1 − p̄ z), which moves p to the origin while preserving all hyperbolic distances; animate p along a geodesic for smooth 'fly-to'. Circle of hyperbolic radius r has circumference 2π·sinh(r), area 2π·(cosh r −1) — exponential, matching tree fan-out.
techniques.what_it_reveals: The ENTIRE form (intro→verse→chorus→bridge…→grain) and the harmonic reduction tree simultaneously: parent/child importance (a Schenkerian/GTTM head note sits near the center, ornaments near the rim), depth of nesting, and sibling balance. By recentering on any chord you see its local context at full detail while the global skeleton stays visible at the rim — impossible in a flat tree where deep levels either overflow or get clipped.
techniques.why_breathtaking: The fisheye disk with glowing geodesic arcs that bow toward the boundary, plus the buttery Möbius 'warp' when you click to recenter (everything slides along circular arcs and the rim spins), is one of the most hypnotic interactions in all of infovis. It feels like piloting through the song's structure.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Self-scaling is intrinsic: the fixed hyperbolic child-step s is the ONLY parameter and it does not depend on tree size — a 50-node form and a 50,000-grain tree use the same s and both fill the disk, because hyperbolic area is unbounded. Wedge subdivision auto-adapts to local branching factor (a node with 12 children gets 12 sub-wedges automatically). No manual zoom range, axis, or resolution needs setting; level-of-detail falls out of the projection (rim nodes are sub-pixel, cull them).
techniques.implementation_notes: Very feasible. Pure CPU layout in O(n); store each node as a complex disk coordinate; the focus transform is one complex Möbius multiply per node per frame (trivial for tens of thousands of nodes). Draw geodesic arcs as quadratic/elliptic arc segments in egui's painter, or push to wgpu for >10k edges. Gotchas: numerical precision near |z|→1 (work in the Klein or hyperboloid model and convert for drawing, or clamp); animate the focus along a geodesic, not a straight Euclidean line, or the motion looks wrong. egui hit-testing must invert the current Möbius to map screen clicks back to node space.

references.title: Lamping, Rao, Pirolli — A Focus+Context Technique Based on Hyperbolic Geometry for Visualizing Large Hierarchies (CHI '95)
Hyperbolic tree — Wikipedia (Möbius navigation, fisheye)
Hyperbolic geometry — Wikipedia (disk metric, sinh/cosh growth)

techniques.name: Poincaré / Sarkar embedding of a harmonic or grain-similarity tree (auto-scaled τ)
techniques.one_line: Build a tree (agglomerative clustering of chords/grains by similarity, or a GTTM/Schenkerian reduction) and embed it into the Poincaré ball with provably low distortion via Sarkar's construction or Riemannian-SGD Poincaré embeddings.
techniques.the_math: Sarkar combinatorial embedding: root at origin; a node's k children are placed on a circle around it, equi-spaced in ANGLE, each at the same hyperbolic radius, then reflected so the parent direction is respected — using the cone subtended by angle 2π/(deg). Edge lengths are scaled by τ>0; choosing τ = ((1+ε)/ε)·log(4·deg_max^{1/(n−1)}) guarantees worst-case multiplicative distortion ≤ 1+ε, giving arbitrarily faithful tree distances. Alternative learned embedding (Nickel–Kiela): minimize a loss over points θ in the ball using Riemannian SGD, θ_{t+1} ← proj(θ_t − η_t · ((1−|θ_t|²)²/4)·∇_E), proj clamps |θ|<1−ε; the (1−|θ|²)²/4 rescaling converts the Euclidean gradient to the Riemannian one under metric λ_θ=2/(1−|θ|²). Curvature-c generalization (Ganea): Möbius add x⊕y, d_c(x,y)=(2/√c)·tanh⁻¹(√c·|(−x)⊕y|), exp/log maps for moving in tangent space.
techniques.what_it_reveals: True hierarchical distance: how 'deep' a chord/grain is (radius = depth/specialness), and which sections are siblings vs cousins — the geometry encodes the reduction. Distances near the rim are huge, so subtly different leaf grains that look adjacent in a flat 2D scatter are correctly pulled far apart; the embedding refuses to falsely cluster things that only share a shallow ancestor.
techniques.why_breathtaking: A cloud of grains/sections that auto-arranges into a luminous fractal tree filling the disk to its edge, root pulsing at center — and because it's an isometric embedding you can fly into any branch forever with the Möbius zoom and detail keeps unfolding. Negative curvature gives that uncanny 'more space than should exist' feeling.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: τ is the self-scaling knob and its optimal value is a CLOSED-FORM function of the data's max degree and node count (formula above) — the embedding literally computes its own scale from the tree it's given. Choosing the ambient dimension n likewise follows n ≥ log(deg_max)+1. You can also measure the data's δ-hyperbolicity (Gromov 4-point condition) first and only switch to hyperbolic when δ is small (data is tree-like), falling back to Euclidean otherwise — a true content-adaptive choice of geometry.
techniques.implementation_notes: Feasible offline-then-realtime. Sarkar's construction is O(n) and deterministic — compute once when the song/album is analyzed, cache disk coords, then render+navigate in realtime exactly like technique #1. Riemannian-SGD embeddings are heavier (seconds for a few thousand nodes) but still fine as a one-time analysis pass; rustfft is irrelevant here but ndarray + a hand-rolled RSGD loop suffice. Gotcha: near-boundary float precision (use f64 or the Lorentz/hyperboloid model internally). The hard part is producing the TREE (clustering grains, or a GTTM reduction), not the embedding.

references.title: Nickel & Kiela — Poincaré Embeddings for Learning Hierarchical Representations (NeurIPS 2017)
Sarkar — Low Distortion Delaunay Embedding of Trees in Hyperbolic Plane
De Sa et al. — Representation Tradeoffs for Hyperbolic Embeddings (scaling factor / distortion)
Ganea, Bécigneul, Hofmann — Hyperbolic Neural Networks (Möbius ops, exp/log maps, curvature c)

techniques.name: Force-directed chord-transition / harmonic Markov network (Fruchterman–Reingold)
techniques.one_line: Nodes = chords (or pitch classes), edges = weighted transition frequencies from the score/analysis; lay out by spring-electrical simulation so harmonically-close chords cluster and pivot chords sit central.
techniques.the_math: Fruchterman–Reingold: optimal edge length k = C·√(Area/|V|). Attractive force along edges f_a(d)=d²/k, repulsive force between all pairs f_r(d)=−k²/d (Eades spring-electrical variant uses f_spring=c_s·log(d/ℓ), f_rep=c_r/d²). Net displacement of vertex u: F_u = Σ_{v} f_r(d_{uv})·û + Σ_{(u,v)∈E} f_a(d_{uv})·û. Simulated-annealing cooling: each step move u by min(|F_u|, T(t))·F_u/|F_u| with temperature T(t) decaying (e.g. T_{t+1}=0.95·T_t). Edge weight = empirical transition probability P(c_j|c_i) from a first-order Markov model of the chord sequence; edge length ∝ 1/weight so frequent transitions pull chords together. Repulsion can be Barnes–Hut–approximated in O(n log n).
techniques.what_it_reveals: Topology of the harmony that the ear cannot tally: which chord is the gravitational center (degree/betweenness centrality = the tonic / most-used pivot), which chord families form COMMUNITIES (Clauset–Newman–Moore modularity = tonal regions / key areas / functional groups), whether the progression is small-world or modular, and harmonic 'entropy' (Shannon / von Neumann over the transition distribution) as a diversity score. It exposes the skeleton of a 200-bar piece as a graph you can read in one glance.
techniques.why_breathtaking: A living constellation that settles from chaos into stable clusters before your eyes, edges glowing in proportion to how often the song makes that move, communities auto-colored — and as the playhead advances you can pulse the currently-sounding chord and trace a comet-trail of the trajectory through the network. It is a fundamentally different, organic modality from grids and tori.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Self-scaling is built in: the ideal length k=C·√(Area/|V|) automatically rescales spacing to the number of distinct chords, so a 6-chord pop song and a 60-chord jazz tune both fill the canvas without manual tuning. Community detection auto-discovers the NUMBER of tonal regions (modularity is optimized, not preset). Edge thickness/length auto-derive from the data's transition statistics; the cooling schedule self-terminates at equilibrium. Optionally let the graph re-layout incrementally as new chords stream in.
techniques.implementation_notes: Highly feasible and the cheapest of all three. Pure CPU, no FFT; a basic FR loop over ~10–200 chord nodes converges in a handful of ms/frame and can animate live in egui. Barnes–Hut quadtree if node count grows (e.g. per-grain nodes). Use petgraph for graph storage and a small CNM/Louvain implementation for communities. Gotchas: layout is non-deterministic (seed the RNG for reproducible pictures), and force-directed graphs can tangle — add a mild gravity-to-center term and clamp displacement to avoid jitter. Drawing thousands of anti-aliased edges is the only place to consider wgpu.

references.title: Fruchterman & Reingold — Graph Drawing by Force-Directed Placement (1991)
Analysis and Visualization of Musical Structure using Networks (chord graphs, centrality, CNM community detection, entropy)
Tonal harmony and the topology of dynamical score networks

techniques.name: 3D hyperbolic (H3) cone-tree of the timbral / form hierarchy
techniques.one_line: Place the hierarchy in the 3D Poincaré ball with children packed on a hemisphere over each parent's cone, projected to a finite ball for a 3D fisheye you orbit and dive through.
techniques.the_math: Lay out on the 3D hyperboloid, project to a finite ball via the projective (Klein–Beltrami) model. Two-pass packing: BOTTOM-UP estimate each parent hemisphere radius by summing child disc areas; TOP-DOWN place children on the parent's hemispherical cap ('sprinkles on an ice-cream cone') sorted by descendant count, largest nearest the pole. Angular increment between adjacent children in a band uses the hyperbolic right-triangle relation tan(φ/2)=sinh(r₁)/sinh(r₂) with r the child-hemisphere hyperbolic radii; start a new concentric band when the current one fills. Navigation = 3D hyperbolic translation moving the clicked node to the ball center (origin/pole) plus a canonical rotation (ancestors left, descendants right), all isometries of H³.
techniques.what_it_reveals: Same hierarchical insight as #1 but with an extra spatial dimension to absorb very wide, very deep trees (e.g. album → song → section → phrase → grain → spectral-cluster) without the 2D disk's angular crowding. The hemisphere packing makes sibling count and subtree mass legible as cap size; diving reveals timbral sub-clusters nested inside sections.
techniques.why_breathtaking: A glowing 3D filigree sphere you orbit, where pushing a node to the center makes the whole structure bloom outward through you — the classic 'flying through hyperbolic space' effect that made H3/Walrus famous. Combined with audio-reactive node pulsing it is jaw-dropping.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Hemisphere radii are COMPUTED from subtree size (sum of child areas), so each parent auto-sizes to its load; band-filling auto-chooses how many concentric rings of children to use based on their radii — no fixed fan-out. The projective model auto-provides level-of-detail (boundary nodes shrink to nothing → cull). As with #1 the single hyperbolic step is size-independent, so the layout fills the ball regardless of tree magnitude.
techniques.implementation_notes: Feasible but the heaviest to render. Layout is O(n) two-pass, computed once. Needs genuine 3D: wgpu/glow with depth-sorted or additive-blended node sprites and arc edges; egui alone is 2D so you draw into a wgpu texture/callback. Gotchas: 3D occlusion (use transparency/additive blending as H3 does), normal transforms in the shader for any lit geometry, and f64 precision near the ball boundary. More engineering than #1–#3; reserve for when a 2D disk genuinely runs out of angular room.

references.title: Munzner — H3: Laying Out Large Directed Graphs in 3D Hyperbolic Space (Stanford thesis)
Munzner & Burchard — Visualizing the Structure of the WWW in 3D Hyperbolic Space

techniques.name: Self-similarity-graph layout of sections/grains (SSM → thresholded graph → spring layout + communities)
techniques.one_line: Turn the audio self-similarity matrix into a weighted graph (frames/grains = nodes, high-similarity pairs = edges) and lay it out by force-direction so repeated material (verses, motifs) physically collapses together.
techniques.the_math: Feature sequence x_1..x_N (chroma, MFCC, or grain spectra). SSM S_{ij} = cos(x_i,x_j) = ⟨x_i,x_j⟩/(|x_i||x_j|) (or 1 − normalized Euclidean). Threshold/kNN to a sparse graph: edge (i,j) iff S_{ij} > τ (or j ∈ kNN(i)), weight w_{ij}=S_{ij}. Diagonal stripes of S (repeats) become dense bipartite-like edge bundles. Layout by spring-electrical (FR forces from technique #3, edge length ∝ 1/w_{ij}). Communities via modularity Q = (1/2m)Σ_{ij}[w_{ij} − k_i k_j/2m]·δ(c_i,c_j), maximized (Louvain/CNM) → automatic SECTION segmentation without preset boundaries. Optional spectral pre-layout: eigenvectors of graph Laplacian L=D−W give a self-scaling coordinate system (Fiedler vector = principal structural axis).
techniques.what_it_reveals: The repetition skeleton as topology rather than a matrix: a chorus that recurs 4× becomes one tight super-cluster with 4 in-edges; an AABA form shows two communities with a bridge node between; novel/through-composed passages show as a long unclustered chain. Centrality flags the most 'representative' grain (a thumbnail/summary seed). This is the same information as the SSM but reorganized so global form is a glance, not a diagonal-stripe-reading exercise.
techniques.why_breathtaking: Watching a 5-minute track's thousands of grain-nodes swirl and then snap into a few bright planets (the repeated sections) joined by faint filaments (transitions) is a genuinely revelatory 'oh THAT'S the shape of this song' moment, and it animates beautifully as it cools.
techniques.autonomous_dimensioning: Deeply self-adapting: the threshold τ can be set automatically from the SSM distribution (e.g. keep top-p% of weights, or per-row kNN so density auto-normalizes across loud/quiet passages). Spring length k=C·√(Area/|V|) auto-scales to grain count. Modularity discovers the number of sections by itself. Spectral/Laplacian embedding chooses its own principal axes (eigenvectors) — the data picks the coordinate frame. Even the number of meaningful dimensions can be read from the Laplacian eigenvalue gap.
techniques.implementation_notes: Feasible; the SSM and features lean on FFT-derived chroma/MFCC (rustfft already present). For an N-frame track the full N×N SSM is O(N²) memory — downsample to beat- or grain-synchronous frames (a few hundred to a few thousand nodes) before building the graph, which also makes the layout realtime. petgraph + Louvain + an FR/Barnes–Hut loop. Gotchas: threshold choice changes the picture (expose it but default to kNN); compute SSM on a coarse grid for interactivity and refine offline. The layout, like all force methods, is non-deterministic — seed for reproducibility.

references.title: Self-similarity matrix — Wikipedia
Foote — Visualizing Music and Audio using Self-Similarity
Analysis and Visualization of Musical Structure using Networks (community detection = segmentation)

key_references.title: Lamping, Rao, Pirolli — A Focus+Context Technique Based on Hyperbolic Geometry (CHI '95 / JVLC 1996)
key_references.why: The seminal hyperbolic browser: the recursive tree layout on the hyperbolic plane, Poincaré-disk fisheye, and Möbius-isometry navigation that the song-form/harmony-tree technique is built on directly.

key_references.title: Nickel & Kiela — Poincaré Embeddings for Learning Hierarchical Representations (NeurIPS 2017)
key_references.why: Establishes that hierarchical/branching data embeds into the Poincaré ball with far lower distortion than Euclidean, and gives the Riemannian-SGD update rule used to learn the harmonic/grain-similarity tree embedding.

key_references.title: Sarkar — Low Distortion Delaunay Embedding of Trees in Hyperbolic Plane
key_references.why: O(n) combinatorial construction with a closed-form scaling factor τ giving ≤1+ε distortion — the autonomous-dimensioning backbone: the layout computes its own scale from the tree's depth and max degree.

key_references.title: Fruchterman & Reingold — Graph Drawing by Force-Directed Placement (1991)
key_references.why: The exact spring-electrical force equations and the self-scaling optimal length k=C·√(Area/|V|) underpinning the chord-transition-network and self-similarity-graph layouts.

key_references.title: Analysis and Visualization of Musical Structure using Networks (2024)
key_references.why: Concrete music application: chord/pitch graphs, centrality = pivot chords, Clauset–Newman–Moore community detection = tonal regions, entropy measures — the insight payload of the graph-network modality.

key_references.title: Munzner — H3: Laying Out Large Directed Graphs in 3D Hyperbolic Space
key_references.why: The 3D Poincaré-ball cone/hemisphere packing (tan(φ/2)=sinh r₁/sinh r₂) and isometric navigation for the 3D hyperbolic timbral-hierarchy technique.

cross_links: Music-theory facet: the Tonnetz/torus and pitch-helix views fold pitch onto FLAT manifolds; this facet supplies the negatively-curved counterpart for the HIERARCHICAL (tree-like) structure those flat views cannot hold without distortion. A chord-transition graph can be drawn ON the Tonnetz lattice as well as force-directed.

Dimensionality-reduction facet: DR (UMAP/t-SNE/PCA) projects timbre to flat 2D/3D and even has hyperbolic variants (hyperbolic UMAP/CO-SNE); the δ-hyperbolicity test here is the principled switch deciding when to use a hyperbolic embedding instead of a Euclidean DR projection. Spectral/Laplacian pre-layout is shared machinery with DR.

Self-similarity / structure facet: the SSM is consumed here as the source of the similarity GRAPH (technique 5); this facet is the network/graph reorganization of the same matrix data, and community detection here doubles as automatic structural segmentation.

Feature-extraction facet: chroma and MFCC/grain spectra (from the FFT pipeline, rustfft) are the node features for both the self-similarity graph and the chord-transition Markov model, so this facet sits downstream of the spectral feature stage.

Color/encoding facet: community labels (tonal regions, repeated sections) and node depth/centrality drive the autonomous color and size mappings, and audio-reactive node pulsing ties the static layout to the live playhead.
